description: Machine-readable enumeration of every EN 16931 / XRechnung business rule the einvoice engine actually asserts, with the syntax it is proven to fire in, its blocking severity, and the official Schematron artifact that differentially proved it. This is the artifact to read to answer "does it run the rules my German ERP needs, in my CI?" — it reflects what the CODE fires (proven by test_coverage_matrix.py against the live rule registries), not aspiration.
generated_by: gen_coverage.py (derived from einvoice.rules + einvoice.rules_xrechnung + differential.py graded sets)

rules.title: An Invoice shall have a Specification identifier (BT-24).
An Invoice shall have an Invoice number (BT-1).
An Invoice shall have an Invoice issue date (BT-2).
An Invoice shall have an Invoice type code (BT-3).
An Invoice shall have an Invoice currency code (BT-5).
An Invoice shall contain the Seller name (BT-27).
An Invoice shall contain the Buyer name (BT-44).
An Invoice shall contain the Seller postal address (BG-5).
The Seller postal address (BG-5) shall contain a Seller country code (BT-40).
An Invoice shall contain the Buyer postal address (BG-8).
The Buyer postal address shall contain a Buyer country code (BT-55).
An Invoice shall have the Sum of Invoice line net amount (BT-106).
An Invoice shall have the Invoice total amount without VAT (BT-109).
An Invoice shall have the Invoice total amount with VAT (BT-112).
An Invoice shall have the Amount due for payment (BT-115).
An Invoice shall have at least one Invoice line (BG-25).
The Payee name (BT-59) shall be provided in the Invoice, if the Payee (BG-10) is different from the Seller (BG-4).
The Seller tax representative name (BT-62) shall be provided in the Invoice, if the Seller (BG-4) has a Seller tax representative party (BG-11).
The Seller tax representative postal address (BG-12) shall be provided in the Invoice, if the Seller (BG-4) has a Seller tax representative party (BG-11).
The Seller tax representative postal address (BG-12) shall contain a Tax representative country code (BT-69), if the Seller (BG-4) has a Seller tax representative party (BG-11).
Each Invoice line shall have an Invoice line identifier (BT-126).
Each Invoice line shall have an Invoiced quantity (BT-129).
An Invoice line (BG-25) shall have an Invoiced quantity unit of measure code (BT-130).
Each Invoice line shall have an Invoice line net amount (BT-131).
Each Invoice line (BG-25) shall contain the Item name (BT-153).
Each Invoice line shall contain the Item net price (BT-146).
The Item net price (BT-146) shall NOT be negative.
The Item gross price (BT-148) shall NOT be negative.
If both Invoicing period start date (BT-73) and end date (BT-74) are given then the end date shall be later or equal to the start date.
If both Invoice line period start date (BT-134) and end date (BT-135) are given then the end date shall be later or equal to the start date.
Each Document level allowance (BG-20) shall have a Document level allowance amount (BT-92).
Each Document level allowance (BG-20) shall have a Document level allowance VAT category code (BT-95).
Each Document level allowance (BG-20) shall have a Document level allowance reason (BT-97) or a Document level allowance reason code (BT-98).
Each Document level charge (BG-21) shall have a Document level charge amount (BT-99).
Each Document level charge (BG-21) shall have a Document level charge VAT category code (BT-102).
Each Document level charge (BG-21) shall have a Document level charge reason (BT-104) or a Document level charge reason code (BT-105).
Each Invoice line allowance (BG-27) shall have an Invoice line allowance amount (BT-136).
Each Invoice line allowance (BG-27) shall have an Invoice line allowance reason (BT-139) or an Invoice line allowance reason code (BT-140).
Each Invoice line charge (BG-28) shall have an Invoice line charge amount (BT-141).
Each Invoice line charge (BG-28) shall have an Invoice line charge reason (BT-144) or an Invoice line charge reason code (BT-145).
Each VAT breakdown (BG-23) shall have a VAT category taxable amount (BT-116).
Each VAT breakdown (BG-23) shall have a VAT category tax amount (BT-117).
Each VAT breakdown (BG-23) shall be defined through a VAT category code (BT-118).
Each VAT breakdown (BG-23) shall have a VAT category rate (BT-119), except if the Invoice is not subject to VAT.
A Payment instruction (BG-16) shall specify the Payment means type code (BT-81).
A Payment account identifier (BT-84) shall be present if Credit transfer (BG-17) information is provided in the Invoice.
The last 4 to 6 digits of the Payment card primary account number (BT-87) shall be present if Payment card information (BG-18) is provided.
Each Additional supporting document (BG-24) shall contain a Supporting document reference (BT-122).
If the VAT accounting currency code (BT-6) is present, then the Invoice total VAT amount in accounting currency (BT-111) shall be provided.
Each Item attribute (BG-32) shall contain an Item attribute name (BT-160) and an Item attribute value (BT-161).
Each Preceding Invoice reference (BG-3) shall contain a Preceding Invoice reference (BT-25).
Each Seller tax representative party (BG-11) shall have a Seller tax representative VAT identifier (BT-63).
Each Deliver to address (BG-15) shall contain a Deliver to country code (BT-80).
If the Payment means type code (BT-81) means SEPA credit transfer, Local credit transfer or Non-SEPA international credit transfer, the Payment account identifier (BT-84) shall be present.
The Seller electronic address (BT-34) shall have a Scheme identifier.
The Buyer electronic address (BT-49) shall have a Scheme identifier.
The Item standard identifier (BT-157) shall have a Scheme identifier.
The Item classification identifier (BT-158) shall have a Scheme identifier.
The document type code (BT-3) MUST be coded per UNTDID 1001.
CurrencyID MUST be coded using ISO 4217 alpha-3.
Invoice currency code (BT-5) MUST be coded using ISO 4217 alpha-3.
Tax currency code (BT-6) MUST be coded using ISO 4217 alpha-3.
Item classification scheme identifier MUST be a UNTDID 7143 code.
Country codes MUST be coded using ISO 3166-1 alpha-2.
Payment means MUST be coded using the UNCL 4461 code list.
Invoice tax categories MUST be coded using the UNCL 5305 subset.
Invoice tax categories MUST be coded using the UNCL 5305 subset.
Coded allowance reasons MUST belong to the UNCL 5189 code list.
Coded charge reasons MUST belong to the UNCL 7161 code list.
Item standard identifier scheme MUST be an ISO 6523 ICD code.
VAT exemption reason code MUST belong to the CEF VATEX list.
Unit code MUST be coded per UN/ECE Rec 20 with Rec 21 extension.
For a MIME code in an attribute use the MIMEMediaType subset.
Value added tax point date (BT-7) and Value added tax point date code (BT-8) are mutually exclusive.
Each Invoice line (BG-25) shall be categorized with an Invoiced item VAT category code (BT-151).

The Seller VAT identifier (BT-31), the Seller tax representative VAT identifier (BT-63) and the Buyer VAT identifier (BT-48) shall have a prefix in accordance with ISO code ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 by which the country of issue may be identified. Nevertheless, Greece may use the prefix 'EL'.
Sum of Invoice line net amount (BT-106) = Σ line net amount (BT-131).
Sum of allowances on document level (BT-107) = Σ Document level allowance amount (BT-92).

cii.reason: core rule not included in the CII differential leg (LEG 3) graded subset; the syntax-agnostic rule body can run over the CII model but CII parity is not asserted for it.

rules.title: Sum of charges on document level (BT-108) = Σ Document level charge amount (BT-99).

cii.reason: core rule not included in the CII differential leg (LEG 3) graded subset; the syntax-agnostic rule body can run over the CII model but CII parity is not asserted for it.

rules.title: Invoice total without VAT (BT-109) = Σ line net (BT-131) − document allowances (BT-107) + document charges (BT-108).
Invoice total VAT amount (BT-110) = Σ VAT category tax amount (BT-117).

cii.reason: official CII context requires a document-currency BT-110 (ram:TaxTotalAmount) which a no-VAT CII invoice legitimately omits, so the assert never fires there; the UBL transcription would over-reject those documents.

rules.title: Invoice total with VAT (BT-112) = total without VAT (BT-109) + total VAT (BT-110).

cii.reason: the CII binding carries an extra GrandTotalAmount = TaxBasisTotalAmount disjunct that holds for a no-VAT invoice with no BT-110; the UBL function has no such disjunct and would over-reject the same documents.

rules.title: Amount due for payment (BT-115) = Invoice total with VAT (BT-112) − Paid amount (BT-113) + Rounding amount (BT-114).
VAT category tax amount (BT-117) = VAT category taxable amount (BT-116) x (VAT category rate (BT-119) / 100), rounded to two decimals.
An Invoice shall at least have one VAT breakdown group (BG-23).
If Invoicing period (BG-14) is used, the Invoicing period start date (BT-73) or the Invoicing period end date (BT-74) shall be filled, or both.
If Invoice line period (BG-26) is used, the Invoice line period start date (BT-134) or the Invoice line period end date (BT-135) shall be filled, or both.
Each Document level allowance (BG-20) shall contain a Document level allowance reason (BT-97) or a Document level allowance reason code (BT-98), or both.
Each Document level charge (BG-21) shall contain a Document level charge reason (BT-104) or a Document level charge reason code (BT-105), or both.
Each Invoice line allowance (BG-27) shall contain an Invoice line allowance reason (BT-139) or an Invoice line allowance reason code (BT-140), or both.
Each Invoice line charge (BG-28) shall contain an Invoice line charge reason (BT-144) or an Invoice line charge reason code (BT-145), or both.
In order for the buyer to automatically identify a supplier, the Seller identifier (BT-29), the Seller legal registration identifier (BT-30) and/or the Seller VAT identifier (BT-31) shall be present.
Max 2 decimals for the Document level allowance amount (BT-92).

cii.reason: core rule not included in the CII differential leg (LEG 3) graded subset; the syntax-agnostic rule body can run over the CII model but CII parity is not asserted for it.

rules.title: Max 2 decimals for the Document level allowance base amount (BT-93).

cii.reason: core rule not included in the CII differential leg (LEG 3) graded subset; the syntax-agnostic rule body can run over the CII model but CII parity is not asserted for it.

rules.title: Max 2 decimals for the Document level charge amount (BT-99).

cii.reason: core rule not included in the CII differential leg (LEG 3) graded subset; the syntax-agnostic rule body can run over the CII model but CII parity is not asserted for it.

rules.title: Max 2 decimals for the Document level charge base amount (BT-100).

cii.reason: core rule not included in the CII differential leg (LEG 3) graded subset; the syntax-agnostic rule body can run over the CII model but CII parity is not asserted for it.

rules.title: Max 2 decimals for the Sum of Invoice line net amount (BT-106).
Max 2 decimals for the Sum of allowances on document level (BT-107).

cii.reason: core rule not included in the CII differential leg (LEG 3) graded subset; the syntax-agnostic rule body can run over the CII model but CII parity is not asserted for it.

rules.title: Max 2 decimals for the Sum of charges on document level (BT-108).

cii.reason: core rule not included in the CII differential leg (LEG 3) graded subset; the syntax-agnostic rule body can run over the CII model but CII parity is not asserted for it.

rules.title: Max 2 decimals for the Invoice total amount without VAT (BT-109).
Max 2 decimals for the Invoice total amount with VAT (BT-112).
Max 2 decimals for the Paid amount (BT-113).

cii.reason: core rule not included in the CII differential leg (LEG 3) graded subset; the syntax-agnostic rule body can run over the CII model but CII parity is not asserted for it.

rules.title: Max 2 decimals for the Rounding amount (BT-114).

cii.reason: core rule not included in the CII differential leg (LEG 3) graded subset; the syntax-agnostic rule body can run over the CII model but CII parity is not asserted for it.

rules.title: Max 2 decimals for the Amount due for payment (BT-115).
Max 2 decimals for the VAT category taxable amount (BT-116).
Max 2 decimals for the VAT category tax amount (BT-117).
Max 2 decimals for the Invoice line net amount (BT-131).
Max 2 decimals for the Invoice line allowance amount (BT-136).
Max 2 decimals for the Invoice line allowance base amount (BT-137). Same line-level allowance context as BR-DEC-24, over ``cbc:BaseAmount`` (UBL) / ``../ram:BasisAmount`` (CII).
Max 2 decimals for the Invoice line charge amount (BT-141). The charge twin of BR-DEC-24 (ChargeIndicator true() / 'true').
Max 2 decimals for the Invoice line charge base amount (BT-142). The charge twin of BR-DEC-25.
'Reverse charge' (AE) items require exactly one AE VAT breakdown (BG-23) row.
An Invoice with a Reverse charge (AE) Invoice line (BT-151) shall carry a Seller identifier AND a Buyer identifier.
An Invoice with a Reverse charge (AE) Document level allowance (BT-95) shall carry a Seller identifier AND a Buyer identifier.
An Invoice with a Reverse charge (AE) Document level charge (BT-102) shall carry a Seller identifier AND a Buyer identifier.
In a Reverse charge (AE) Invoice line the Invoiced item VAT rate (BT-152) shall be 0.
In a Reverse charge (AE) Document level allowance the allowance VAT rate (BT-96) shall be 0.
In a Reverse charge (AE) Document level charge the charge VAT rate (BT-103) shall be 0.
The Reverse charge (AE) VAT breakdown taxable amount (BT-116) shall equal the exact sum of AE line nets − AE allowances + AE charges.
The VAT category tax amount (BT-117) in a Reverse charge (AE) VAT breakdown shall equal 0.

A VAT breakdown (BG-23) with a Reverse charge (AE) VAT category code (BT-118) SHALL have a VAT exemption reason code (BT-121) meaning 'Reverse charge' or the reason text (BT-120) 'Reverse charge' — the presence-required shape shared with BR-E-10.
IGIC (L) items and the VAT breakdown (BG-23) must agree.

An IGIC (L) Invoice line (BT-151) requires the Seller VAT identifier (BT-31), Seller tax registration id (BT-32) and/or Seller tax representative VAT id (BT-63) — both official disjuncts are VAT-scoped (the BR-Z/E-02 symmetric shape, not BR-S-02's scheme-agnostic tail).
An IGIC (L) Document level allowance (BT-95) requires the Seller VAT identifier disjunct (same shape as BR-AF-02).
An IGIC (L) Document level charge (BT-102) requires the Seller VAT identifier disjunct.
In an IGIC (L) Invoice line the Invoiced item VAT rate (BT-152) shall be 0 (zero) or greater than zero.
In an IGIC (L) Document level allowance the allowance VAT rate (BT-96) shall be 0 (zero) or greater than zero.
In an IGIC (L) Document level charge the charge VAT rate (BT-103) shall be 0 (zero) or greater than zero.

For each different value of VAT category rate (BT-119) where the VAT category code (BT-118) is 'IGIC', the VAT category taxable amount (BT-116) shall equal the sum of Invoice line net amounts (BT-131) plus document level charge amounts (BT-99) minus document level allowance amounts (BT-92) where the VAT category code is 'IGIC' and the VAT rate equals BT-119.

cii.reason: the CII artifact binds this assert to the ram:ApplicableTradeTax ROW — unlike BR-S-08, whose context node is the ram:CategoryCode CHILD — so the test's ../ram:RateApplicablePercent resolves against the header settlement (no such children) and 'every $rate in ()' is vacuously true: the shipped assert can never fire. The engine asserts the intended per-rate round2 bucket sum on CII anyway (deliberate strictness).

rules.title: The VAT category tax amount (BT-117) in an IGIC (L) VAT breakdown shall equal the VAT category taxable amount (BT-116) multiplied by the VAT category rate (BT-119).

cii.reason: the official CII artifact ships this assert as test="true()" — a tautology that can never fire, whatever the arithmetic — so CII parity is impossible for a real check; the engine asserts the UBL binding's taxable × rate ±1 band on both syntaxes instead (deliberate strictness).

rules.title: A VAT breakdown (BG-23) with an IGIC (L) VAT category code (BT-118) shall not have a VAT exemption reason code (BT-121) or VAT exemption reason text (BT-120).
IPSI (M) items and the VAT breakdown (BG-23) must agree.

An IPSI (M) Invoice line (BT-151) requires the Seller VAT identifier (BT-31), Seller tax registration id (BT-32) and/or Seller tax representative VAT id (BT-63) — both official disjuncts are VAT-scoped (the BR-Z/E/AF-02 symmetric shape, not BR-S-02's scheme-agnostic tail).
An IPSI (M) Document level allowance (BT-95) requires the Seller VAT identifier disjunct (same shape as BR-AG-02).
An IPSI (M) Document level charge (BT-102) requires the Seller VAT identifier disjunct.
In an IPSI (M) Invoice line the Invoiced item VAT rate (BT-152) shall be 0 (zero) or greater than zero.
In an IPSI (M) Document level allowance the allowance VAT rate (BT-96) shall be 0 (zero) or greater than zero.
In an IPSI (M) Document level charge the charge VAT rate (BT-103) shall be 0 (zero) or greater than zero.

For each different value of VAT category rate (BT-119) where the VAT category code (BT-118) is 'IPSI', the VAT category taxable amount (BT-116) shall equal the sum of Invoice line net amounts (BT-131) plus document level charge amounts (BT-99) minus document level allowance amounts (BT-92) where the VAT category code is 'IPSI' and the VAT rate equals BT-119.

cii.reason: the CII artifact repeats the BR-AF-08 binding defect for the IPSI (M) family: the assert is bound to the ram:ApplicableTradeTax ROW, so its ../ram:RateApplicablePercent is empty and 'every $rate in ()' is vacuously true — the shipped assert can never fire. The engine asserts the intended per-rate round2 bucket sum on CII anyway (deliberate strictness).

rules.title: The VAT category tax amount (BT-117) in an IPSI (M) VAT breakdown shall equal the VAT category taxable amount (BT-116) multiplied by the VAT category rate (BT-119).

cii.reason: the official CII artifact ships this assert as test="true()" — the same never-firing tautology as BR-AF-09 — so CII parity is impossible for a real check; the engine asserts the UBL binding's taxable × rate ±1 band on both syntaxes instead (deliberate strictness).

rules.title: A VAT breakdown (BG-23) with an IPSI (M) VAT category code (BT-118) shall not have a VAT exemption reason code (BT-121) or VAT exemption reason text (BT-120).
An Invoice where the VAT category code (BT-151, BT-95 or BT-102) is 'Split payment' shall be a domestic Italian invoice.
An Invoice with a 'Split payment' (B) VAT category code (BT-151, BT-95, BT-118 or BT-102) shall not also contain a 'Standard rated' (S) VAT category code.
'Exempt from VAT' (E) items require exactly one E VAT breakdown (BG-23) row.
An Exempt (E) Invoice line (BT-151) requires the Seller VAT identifier / tax registration id / tax representative VAT id.
An Exempt (E) Document level allowance (BT-95) requires the Seller VAT identifier disjunct.
An Exempt (E) Document level charge (BT-102) requires the Seller VAT identifier disjunct.
In an Exempt (E) Invoice line the Invoiced item VAT rate (BT-152) shall be 0.
In an Exempt (E) Document level allowance the allowance VAT rate (BT-96) shall be 0.
In an Exempt (E) Document level charge the charge VAT rate (BT-103) shall be 0.

The Exempt (E) VAT breakdown taxable amount (BT-116) shall equal the sum of E line net amounts − E allowances + E charges (exact on UBL; the ±1 band around the round2 bucket sums on CII — see :func:`_breakdown_taxable_sum_mismatch`).
The VAT category tax amount (BT-117) in an Exempt (E) VAT breakdown shall equal 0.

A VAT breakdown (BG-23) with an Exempt from VAT (E) VAT category code (BT-118) SHALL have a VAT exemption reason code (BT-121) or text (BT-120) — the presence-required mirror image of BR-Z-10/BR-S-10.
'Export outside the EU' (G) items require exactly one G VAT breakdown (BG-23) row.
An Invoice with an Export outside the EU (G) Invoice line (BT-151) shall carry a VAT-scoped Seller identifier (BT-31/BT-63).
An Invoice with an Export outside the EU (G) Document level allowance (BT-95) shall carry a VAT-scoped Seller identifier.
An Invoice with an Export outside the EU (G) Document level charge (BT-102) shall carry a VAT-scoped Seller identifier.
In an Export outside the EU (G) Invoice line the Invoiced item VAT rate (BT-152) shall be 0.
In an Export outside the EU (G) Document level allowance the allowance VAT rate (BT-96) shall be 0.
In an Export outside the EU (G) Document level charge the charge VAT rate (BT-103) shall be 0.

The Export outside the EU (G) VAT breakdown taxable amount (BT-116) shall equal the sum of G line nets − G allowances + G charges (exact on UBL; the ±1 band around the round2 bucket sums on CII — see :func:`_breakdown_taxable_sum_mismatch`).
The VAT category tax amount (BT-117) in an Export outside the EU (G) VAT breakdown shall equal 0.

A VAT breakdown (BG-23) with an Export outside the EU (G) VAT category code (BT-118) SHALL have a VAT exemption reason code (BT-121) or text (BT-120) — the presence-required shape shared with BR-E-10.
'Intra-community supply' (K) items require exactly one K VAT breakdown (BG-23) row.
An Invoice with an Intra-community supply (K) Invoice line (BT-151) shall carry a VAT-scoped Seller identifier AND the Buyer VAT identifier.
An Invoice with an Intra-community supply (K) Document level allowance (BT-95) shall carry a VAT-scoped Seller identifier AND the Buyer VAT identifier.
An Invoice with an Intra-community supply (K) Document level charge (BT-102) shall carry a VAT-scoped Seller identifier AND the Buyer VAT identifier.
In an Intra-community supply (K) Invoice line the Invoiced item VAT rate (BT-152) shall be 0.
In an Intra-community supply (K) Document level allowance the allowance VAT rate (BT-96) shall be 0.
In an Intra-community supply (K) Document level charge the charge VAT rate (BT-103) shall be 0.
The Intra-community supply (K) VAT breakdown taxable amount (BT-116) shall equal the exact sum of K line nets − K allowances + K charges.
The VAT category tax amount (BT-117) in an Intra-community supply (K) VAT breakdown shall equal 0.
A VAT breakdown (BG-23) with the VAT category code (BT-118) "Intra-community supply" (K) SHALL have a VAT exemption reason code (BT-121) or text (BT-120) — the K twin of BR-E-10 / BR-AE-10.
In an Invoice with an Intra-community supply (K) VAT breakdown (BG-23) the Actual delivery date (BT-72) or the Invoicing period (BG-14) shall not be blank.
In an Invoice with an Intra-community supply (K) VAT breakdown (BG-23) the Deliver to country code (BT-80) shall not be blank.
'Not subject to VAT' (O) items require exactly one O VAT breakdown (BG-23) row.

cii.reason: core rule not included in the CII differential leg (LEG 3) graded subset; the syntax-agnostic rule body can run over the CII model but CII parity is not asserted for it.

rules.title: Standard-rated (S) items and the VAT breakdown must agree.

cii.reason: the CII binding is a weak one-directional count that does not flag an orphan Standard-rated breakdown; the UBL biconditional would over-fire on such CII invoices.

rules.title: An Invoice with a Standard-rated (S) Invoice line (BT-151) shall contain the Seller VAT Identifier (BT-31), Seller tax registration id (BT-32) and/or Seller tax representative VAT id (BT-63).
An Invoice with a Standard-rated (S) Document level allowance (BT-95) shall contain the Seller VAT id / tax registration id / tax rep VAT id (same seller disjunct as BR-S-02).

rules.title: In an Invoice line where the Invoiced item VAT category code (BT-151) is 'Standard rated' the Invoiced item VAT rate (BT-152) shall be greater than zero.
In a Document level allowance where the allowance VAT category code (BT-95) is 'Standard rated' the allowance VAT rate (BT-96) shall be greater than zero.

cii.reason: core rule not included in the CII differential leg (LEG 3) graded subset; the syntax-agnostic rule body can run over the CII model but CII parity is not asserted for it.

rules.title: In a Document level charge where the charge VAT category code (BT-102) is 'Standard rated' the charge VAT rate (BT-103) shall be greater than zero.

cii.reason: core rule not included in the CII differential leg (LEG 3) graded subset; the syntax-agnostic rule body can run over the CII model but CII parity is not asserted for it.

rules.title: For each different value of VAT category rate (BT-119) where the VAT category code (BT-118) is "Standard rated", the VAT category taxable amount (BT-116) shall equal the sum of Invoice line net amounts (BT-131) plus document level charge amounts (BT-99) minus document level allowance amounts (BT-92) where the VAT category code is "Standard rated" and the VAT rate equals BT-119.
The VAT category tax amount (BT-117) in a Standard-rated (S) VAT breakdown shall equal the VAT category taxable amount (BT-116) x the VAT category rate (BT-119).
A VAT breakdown (BG-23) with a Standard rated (S) VAT category code (BT-118) shall not have a VAT exemption reason text (BT-120) or code (BT-121).
If any line/allowance/charge is Zero rated (Z), the VAT breakdown must contain exactly one Zero rated category.

cii.reason: core rule not included in the CII differential leg (LEG 3) graded subset; the syntax-agnostic rule body can run over the CII model but CII parity is not asserted for it.

rules.title: A Zero-rated (Z) Document level charge (BT-102) requires the Seller VAT identifier disjunct.

rules.title: In a Zero-rated (Z) Invoice line the Invoiced item VAT rate (BT-152) shall be 0.

cii.reason: core rule not included in the CII differential leg (LEG 3) graded subset; the syntax-agnostic rule body can run over the CII model but CII parity is not asserted for it.

rules.title: In a Zero-rated (Z) Document level allowance the allowance VAT rate (BT-96) shall be 0.

cii.reason: core rule not included in the CII differential leg (LEG 3) graded subset; the syntax-agnostic rule body can run over the CII model but CII parity is not asserted for it.

rules.title: In a Zero-rated (Z) Document level charge the charge VAT rate (BT-103) shall be 0.

rules.title: An invoice must contain PAYMENT INSTRUCTIONS (BG-16).
SELLER CONTACT (BG-6) must be transmitted.
Seller city (BT-37) must be transmitted (non-empty).
Seller post code (BT-38) must be transmitted (non-empty).
Seller contact point (BT-41) must be transmitted (non-empty).
Seller contact telephone number (BT-42) must be transmitted.
Seller contact email address (BT-43) must be transmitted.
Buyer city (BT-52) must be transmitted (non-empty).
Buyer post code (BT-53) must be transmitted (non-empty).
Deliver to city (BT-77) must be transmitted when DELIVER TO ADDRESS (BG-15) is present.
Deliver to post code (BT-78) must be transmitted when DELIVER TO ADDRESS (BG-15) is present.
VAT category rate (BT-119) must be transmitted (non-empty) in every top-level VAT breakdown row.
Buyer reference (BT-10) must be transmitted (non-empty).
If VAT category codes S/Z/E/AE/K/G/L/M are used, one of Seller VAT identifier (BT-31), Seller tax registration identifier (BT-32) or SELLER TAX REPRESENTATIVE PARTY (BG-11) must be present.
Skonto (cash-discount) lines in Payment terms (BT-20).

cii.reason: Skonto grammar in the BT-20 payment-terms free text — a structure the syntax-agnostic core model omits.

rules.title: With payment means code 58 (SEPA credit transfer), BT-84 should be a correct IBAN (official regex + mod-97 transcription).

cii.reason: IBAN mod-97 on a credit-transfer payment-means IBANID — the CII payment-means node set and IBAN digits are not in the core model.

rules.title: With payment means code 59 (SEPA direct debit), BT-91 should be a correct IBAN.

cii.reason: IBAN mod-97 on a payment-means IBANID — not carried by the core model (see BR-DE-19).

rules.title: BT-24 should be the XRechnung specification identifier (CIUS, extension or CVD variant) — untrimmed string equality.
The filename attribute of all EmbeddedDocumentBinaryObject elements must be unique (across cac:AdditionalDocumentReference).

cii.reason: unique attachment filename check over every EmbeddedDocumentBinaryObject/@filename — not carried.

rules.title: Codes 30/58 (credit transfer) require CREDIT TRANSFER (BG-17).

cii.reason: payment-means type-code group check keyed on SpecifiedTradeSettlementPaymentMeans TypeCode and its financial-account children — not carried.

rules.title: Codes 30/58 forbid PAYMENT CARD (BG-18) and DIRECT DEBIT (BG-19).

cii.reason: payment-means type-code group check keyed on SpecifiedTradeSettlementPaymentMeans TypeCode and its financial-account children — not carried.

rules.title: Codes 48/54/55 (card) require PAYMENT CARD INFORMATION (BG-18).

cii.reason: payment-means type-code group check (card) — not carried.

rules.title: Codes 48/54/55 forbid CREDIT TRANSFER (BG-17) and DIRECT DEBIT (BG-19).

cii.reason: payment-means type-code group check (card) — not carried.

rules.title: Code 59 (direct debit) requires DIRECT DEBIT (BG-19).

cii.reason: payment-means type-code group check (direct debit) — not carried.

rules.title: Code 59 forbids CREDIT TRANSFER (BG-17) and PAYMENT CARD (BG-18).

cii.reason: payment-means type-code group check (direct debit) — not carried.

rules.title: Type code 384 (Corrected invoice) should carry a PRECEDING INVOICE REFERENCE (BG-3).
BT-42 should contain at least three digits. Evaluated per seller Contact; an ABSENT telephone normalizes to '' and fires too.
BT-43 should look like an email address (exactly one '@', flanked per the official regex).
DIRECT DEBIT (BG-19) requires the Bank assigned creditor identifier (BT-90: a SEPA-scheme PartyIdentification of the seller or payee).

cii.reason: BT-90/BT-91 with DIRECT DEBIT (BG-19), reconstructed from mandate / creditor-reference / IBAN presence — not in the core model.

rules.title: DIRECT DEBIT (BG-19) requires the Debited account identifier (BT-91).

cii.reason: BT-90/BT-91 with DIRECT DEBIT (BG-19) — not carried (see BR-DE-30).

rules.title: An invoice should state the delivery/service date via BT-72 (Actual delivery date), BG-14 (Invoicing period) or a BG-26 (Invoice line period) on EVERY line.

Every 'Attached Document' binary object (BT-125) must use an Extension-allowed MIME code. Context is cbc:EmbeddedDocumentBinaryObject anywhere in the document; the extra allowance over EN 8.2 is application/xml. An absent @mimeCode also fires (empty node-set).

cii.reason: part of the KoSIT XRechnung EXTENSION layer (UBL CustomizationID only); no CII extension profile is in scope.

rules.title: The 'Invoice line net amount' (BT-131) of an INVOICE LINE (BG-25) or a SUB INVOICE LINE (BG-DEX-01) should equal the sum of the directly nested SUB INVOICE LINEs' net amounts.

cii.reason: part of the KoSIT XRechnung EXTENSION layer (UBL CustomizationID only); no CII extension profile is in scope.

rules.title: A SUB INVOICE LINE (BG-DEX-01) must carry exactly one SUB INVOICE LINE VAT INFORMATION (BG-DEX-06) — i.e. its Item must have exactly one cac:ClassifiedTaxCategory. Fires if any sub-line item has 0 or >1.

cii.reason: part of the KoSIT XRechnung EXTENSION layer (UBL CustomizationID only); no CII extension profile is in scope.

rules.title: Any scheme identifier on a Party identifier (cac:Party Identification/cbc:ID) must be an ISO 6523 ICD (extension) code — or 'SEPA' when the identifier belongs to the Seller or the Payee.

cii.reason: part of the KoSIT XRechnung EXTENSION layer (UBL CustomizationID only); no CII extension profile is in scope.

rules.title: Any scheme identifier on a legal registration identifier (cac:PartyLegalEntity/cbc:CompanyID, BT-30/BT-47) must be an ISO 6523 ICD (extension) code.

cii.reason: part of the KoSIT XRechnung EXTENSION layer (UBL CustomizationID only); no CII extension profile is in scope.

rules.title: Any scheme identifier on an item standard identifier (cac:StandardItemIdentification/cbc:ID, BT-157) must be an ISO 6523 ICD (extension) code.

cii.reason: part of the KoSIT XRechnung EXTENSION layer (UBL CustomizationID only); no CII extension profile is in scope.

rules.title: Any scheme identifier on an Endpoint identifier (cbc:Endpoint ID, BT-34/BT-49) must belong to the CEF EAS (extension) code list.

cii.reason: part of the KoSIT XRechnung EXTENSION layer (UBL CustomizationID only); no CII extension profile is in scope.

rules.title: Any scheme identifier on a Deliver-to location identifier (cac:DeliveryLocation/cbc:ID, BT-71) must be an ISO 6523 ICD (extension) code.

cii.reason: part of the KoSIT XRechnung EXTENSION layer (UBL CustomizationID only); no CII extension profile is in scope.

rules.title: Amount due for payment (BT-115) = Invoice total amount with VAT (BT-112) - Paid amount (BT-113) + Rounding amount (BT-114) + Σ Third party payment amount (BT-DEX-002).

cii.reason: part of the KoSIT XRechnung EXTENSION layer (UBL CustomizationID only); no CII extension profile is in scope.

rules.title: 'Third party payment type' (BT-DEX-001, cbc:ID) must be present (non-empty) in every THIRD PARTY PAYMENT group (BG-DEX-09).

cii.reason: part of the KoSIT XRechnung EXTENSION layer (UBL CustomizationID only); no CII extension profile is in scope.

rules.title: 'Third party payment amount' (BT-DEX-002, cbc:PaidAmount) must be present (non-empty) in every THIRD PARTY PAYMENT group (BG-DEX-09).

cii.reason: part of the KoSIT XRechnung EXTENSION layer (UBL CustomizationID only); no CII extension profile is in scope.

rules.title: 'Third party payment description' (BT-DEX-003, cbc:InstructionID) must be present (non-empty) in every THIRD PARTY PAYMENT group (BG-DEX-09).

cii.reason: part of the KoSIT XRechnung EXTENSION layer (UBL CustomizationID only); no CII extension profile is in scope.

rules.title: 'Third party payment amount' (BT-DEX-002) may carry at most 2 fractional digits: string-length(substring-after(cbc:PaidAmount, '.')) <= 2 (no '.' -> '' -> length 0 -> holds).

cii.reason: part of the KoSIT XRechnung EXTENSION layer (UBL CustomizationID only); no CII extension profile is in scope.

rules.title: The currency of 'Third party payment amount' (BT-DEX-002) must equal BT-5 (Invoice currency code): cbc:PaidAmount/@currencyID = parent::node()/cbc:DocumentCurrencyCode. A missing @currencyID or a missing DocumentCurrencyCode makes the node-set comparison false -> fires.

cii.reason: part of the KoSIT XRechnung EXTENSION layer (UBL CustomizationID only); no CII extension profile is in scope.

rules.title: A CVD invoice must transmit the 'Contract reference' (BT-12, cac:ContractDocumentReference/cbc:ID, non-empty).
A CVD invoice must transmit the 'Tender or lot reference' (BT-17, cac:OriginatorDocumentReference/cbc:ID, non-empty).

A CVD invoice must contain at least one INVOICE LINE (BG-25) whose Item carries an 'Item classification identifier' (BT-158) with scheme identifier 'CVD' AND an 'Item attribute name' (BT-160) with the value 'cva' — both on the SAME cac:Item.

In a CVD invoice, an 'Item classification identifier' (BT-158) with scheme identifier 'CVD' must contain one of the permitted vehicle categories M1, M2, M3, N1, N2, N3 (normalize-space comparison, per the official test).

In a CVD invoice, when the 'Item attribute name' (BT-160) within ITEM ATTRIBUTES (BG-32) is 'cva', the 'Item attribute value' (BT-161) must be one of 'clean', 'zero-emission', 'other' (normalize-space comparison; an absent cbc:Value normalizes to '' and fires).

In a CVD invoice line whose Item carries an 'Item classification identifier' (BT-158) with scheme identifier 'CVD', exactly one 'Item attribute name' (BT-160) with the value 'cva' must be present on that Item.

In a CVD invoice line whose Item carries an 'Item attribute name' (BT-160) with the value 'cva', exactly one 'Item classification identifier' (BT-158) with scheme identifier 'CVD' must be present on that Item.
The 'External document location' (BT-124) must be an absolute URL with a valid scheme.

When the 'Item price base quantity' (BT-149) is present in BOTH GrossPriceProductTradePrice and NetPriceProductTradePrice of a line, the values must be identical, and when both carry a unit of measure code (BT-150) the unit codes must be identical too.

ubl.reason: the vendored KoSIT XRechnung UBL artifact carries NO assert with this id — the rule exists only in the CII binding, so a UBL proof is impossible by construction (verified by a live XML parse in test_coverage_gap.py).

rules.title: In a CVD invoice, the scheme identifier of every 'Item classification identifier' (BT-158) must come from the code list UNTDID 7143 (extended with 'CVD'). Official membership test is contains() over the space-flanked official list — see :func:`_untdid_7143_cvd_ok`.
Business process MUST be provided.
VAT accounting currency code MUST be different from invoice currency code when provided.
Document MUST not contain empty elements.
Buyer electronic address MUST be provided.
Seller electronic address MUST be provided.
Allowance/charge amount must equal base amount * percentage/100 if base amount and percentage exists.
Allowance/charge base amount MUST be provided when allowance/charge percentage is provided.
Allowance/charge percentage MUST be provided when allowance/charge base amount is provided.
Allowance/charge ChargeIndicator value MUST equal 'true' or 'false'.

cii.note: the CII artifact splits this rule into two asserts (SpecifiedTradeAllowanceCharge and AppliedTradeAllowanceCharge contexts); BOTH are graded individually on LEG 4.

rules.title: Charge on price level is NOT allowed. Only value 'false' allowed.
Item net price MUST equal (Gross price - Allowance amount) when gross price is provided.
Only one tax total with tax subtotals MUST be provided.
Only one tax total without tax subtotals MUST be provided when tax currency code is provided.
Invoice total VAT amount and Invoice total VAT amount in accounting currency MUST have the same operational sign.
Mandate reference MUST be provided for direct debit.
Element Document reference can only be used for Invoice line object.
Start date of line period MUST be within invoice period. (Line start >= document invoice-period start.)
End date of line period MUST be within invoice period. (Line end <= document invoice-period end.)
Invoice line net amount MUST equal (Invoiced quantity * (Item net price/item price base quantity) + Sum of invoice line charge amount - sum of invoice line allowance amount.
Base quantity MUST be a positive number above zero.
Unit code of price base quantity MUST be same as invoiced quantity.

exclusions.description: Rules deliberately NOT counted as coverage, documented so the matrix is honest about its boundaries.

vacuous.reason: vacuous in official Schematron (predicate references a non-existent child of cbc:TaxAmount) — never fires
vacuous in official Schematron (same defect, TaxCurrencyCode) — never fires

official_tautology.official_text: Document level allowance reason code (BT-98) and Document level allowance reason (BT-97) shall indicate the same type of allowance.
official_tautology.reason: shipped as the literal tautology test="true()" in BOTH CEN preprocessed artifacts (UBL and CII) — the assert is always satisfied and can never fire, whatever the invoice contains, so no implementation of this rule could ever be differentially proven against the official Schematron; excluded by construction rather than implemented on faith.

official_tautology.official_text: Document level charge reason code (BT-105) and Document level charge reason (BT-104) shall indicate the same type of charge.
official_tautology.reason: shipped as the literal tautology test="true()" in BOTH CEN preprocessed artifacts (UBL and CII) — the assert is always satisfied and can never fire, whatever the invoice contains, so no implementation of this rule could ever be differentially proven against the official Schematron; excluded by construction rather than implemented on faith.

official_tautology.official_text: Invoice line allowance reason code (BT-140) and Invoice line allowance reason (BT-139) shall indicate the same type of allowance reason.
official_tautology.reason: shipped as the literal tautology test="true()" in BOTH CEN preprocessed artifacts (UBL and CII) — the assert is always satisfied and can never fire, whatever the invoice contains, so no implementation of this rule could ever be differentially proven against the official Schematron; excluded by construction rather than implemented on faith.

official_tautology.official_text: Invoice line charge reason code (BT-145) and Invoice line charge reason (BT-144) shall indicate the same type of charge reason.
official_tautology.reason: shipped as the literal tautology test="true()" in BOTH CEN preprocessed artifacts (UBL and CII) — the assert is always satisfied and can never fire, whatever the invoice contains, so no implementation of this rule could ever be differentially proven against the official Schematron; excluded by construction rather than implemented on faith.

codelist_not_asserted.reason: VAT-point date code. Not asserted: the UBL binding (cac:InvoicePeriod/cbc:DescriptionCode, UNTDID 2005 subset 3/35/432) and the CII binding (ram:DueDateTypeCode, UNTDID 2475 subset 5/29/72) use DIFFERENT code lists at DIFFERENT context nodes; the per-syntax value set is not yet carried.

Object/document reference identifier scheme (UNTDID 1153). Not asserted: the UBL context is scoped to a DocumentReference with cbc:DocumentTypeCode='130' (a predicate the model does not carry) and the CII context is ram:ReferenceTypeCode — two distinct bindings, deferred.
Subject code (UNTDID 4451). CII-only rule (ram:SubjectCode) with no UBL counterpart, so it falls outside the both-syntaxes codelist scope; not asserted.

Party identifier scheme in the ISO 6523 ICD list. Not asserted: a broad party-identification scheme surface across many context nodes; the 243-code ICD enumeration IS inlined in the .sch, but the authoritative ISO 6523 register in corpus is a PDF (codelist/iso6523/ICD-list.pdf), so it is deferred rather than partially asserted.
Party registration identifier scheme in the ISO 6523 ICD list. Not asserted: same ICD surface as BR-CL-10 bound to PartyLegalEntity/CompanyID / a scoped ram:ID; deferred.
Item origin country code (ISO 3166-1). Not asserted: the same code lists as BR-CL-14 but a distinct context node (cac:OriginCountry / ram:OriginTradeCountry) the model does not yet collect.

Electronic-address scheme identifier (CEF EAS). Not asserted: the EAS code set IS inlined in the .sch (cbc:EndpointID/@schemeID / ram:URIID/@schemeID), but the endpoint scheme-identifier parser surface is deferred; the authoritative register is the ISO 6523 PDF in corpus, not a machine-readable list. The set is NOT fabricated from the PDF.

Delivery-location identifier scheme (ISO 6523 ICD). Not asserted: the same ICD list as BR-CL-21 bound to a different context node (cac:DeliveryLocation/cbc:ID / ram:ShipToTradeParty/ram:GlobalID @schemeID); deferred.

cii_core_out_of_scope.reason: official CII context requires a document-currency BT-110 (ram:TaxTotalAmount) which a no-VAT CII invoice legitimately omits, so the assert never fires there; the UBL transcription would over-reject those documents.

the CII binding carries an extra GrandTotalAmount = TaxBasisTotalAmount disjunct that holds for a no-VAT invoice with no BT-110; the UBL function has no such disjunct and would over-reject the same documents.

the CII artifact binds this assert to the ram:ApplicableTradeTax ROW — unlike BR-S-08, whose context node is the ram:CategoryCode CHILD — so the test's ../ram:RateApplicablePercent resolves against the header settlement (no such children) and 'every $rate in ()' is vacuously true: the shipped assert can never fire. The engine asserts the intended per-rate round2 bucket sum on CII anyway (deliberate strictness).

the official CII artifact ships this assert as test="true()" — a tautology that can never fire, whatever the arithmetic — so CII parity is impossible for a real check; the engine asserts the UBL binding's taxable × rate ±1 band on both syntaxes instead (deliberate strictness).

the CII artifact repeats the BR-AF-08 binding defect for the IPSI (M) family: the assert is bound to the ram:ApplicableTradeTax ROW, so its ../ram:RateApplicablePercent is empty and 'every $rate in ()' is vacuously true — the shipped assert can never fire. The engine asserts the intended per-rate round2 bucket sum on CII anyway (deliberate strictness).

the official CII artifact ships this assert as test="true()" — the same never-firing tautology as BR-AF-09 — so CII parity is impossible for a real check; the engine asserts the UBL binding's taxable × rate ±1 band on both syntaxes instead (deliberate strictness).
the CII binding is a weak one-directional count that does not flag an orphan Standard-rated breakdown; the UBL biconditional would over-fire on such CII invoices.

cii_de_out_of_scope.reason: Skonto grammar in the BT-20 payment-terms free text — a structure the syntax-agnostic core model omits.
IBAN mod-97 on a credit-transfer payment-means IBANID — the CII payment-means node set and IBAN digits are not in the core model.
IBAN mod-97 on a payment-means IBANID — not carried by the core model (see BR-DE-19).
unique attachment filename check over every EmbeddedDocumentBinaryObject/@filename — not carried.
payment-means type-code group check keyed on SpecifiedTradeSettlementPaymentMeans TypeCode and its financial-account children — not carried.
payment-means type-code group check keyed on SpecifiedTradeSettlementPaymentMeans TypeCode and its financial-account children — not carried.
payment-means type-code group check (card) — not carried.
payment-means type-code group check (card) — not carried.
payment-means type-code group check (direct debit) — not carried.
payment-means type-code group check (direct debit) — not carried.
BT-90/BT-91 with DIRECT DEBIT (BG-19), reconstructed from mandate / creditor-reference / IBAN presence — not in the core model.
BT-90/BT-91 with DIRECT DEBIT (BG-19) — not carried (see BR-DE-30).

peppol.note: Scoped honestly: the engine asserts ALL 21 canonical PEPPOL-EN16931-R* rules that KoSIT ships inside the official XRechnung Schematron artifact, in both bindings (see the peppol_kosit_family section and the rule table; each is differentially proven per binding). This is NOT full Peppol BIS Billing 3.0 support: the OpenPeppol ruleset proper (its own Schematron + test corpus) is a separate, not-vendored artifact, and nothing beyond the KoSIT-vendored asserts is claimed. The family enumeration stays machine-checked in peppol_kosit_family, recomputed live by test_coverage_gap.py, so an artifact bump that adds a new Peppol assert reopens the worklist automatically.

gap.description: Machine-checked complement of the rule table: for each CEN EN 16931 artifact, every official BR-* assert id that is NEITHER implemented by the engine NOR listed as a deliberate exclusion — extracted by a real XML parse of sch:assert/@id from the vendored preprocessed Schematron, with the official rule text carried verbatim. fireable_missing further subtracts any missing assert the artifact itself ships as a literal test="true()" tautology (rules that can never fire officially belong to the official_tautology exclusion class, not this worklist). test_coverage_gap.py recomputes this live from the .sch files, fails on any drift, and asserts fireable_missing == 0 for every universe — so the gap can neither be hidden nor go stale, and any future artifact bump that turns a tautology into a real rule reopens the worklist automatically.

peppol_kosit_family.label: the Peppol-derived rules KoSIT ships inside the official XRechnung Schematron artifact
peppol_kosit_family.description: Machine-checked enumeration of the Peppol-derived rules KoSIT ships inside the official XRechnung Schematron artifact (the peppol-* patterns of the vendored KoSIT XRechnung Schematron v2.5.0), extracted by a real XML parse of sch:assert/@id from BOTH binding artifacts. This is NOT full Peppol BIS Billing 3.0 support: the OpenPeppol ruleset proper (its own Schematron and test corpus) is a separate, not-vendored artifact, and nothing beyond the asserts KoSIT ships is claimed. Implemented ids are read from the live einvoice.rules_peppol registries and are differentially proven per binding (LEG 2 / LEG 4); the remainder is the explicit known_open_worklist below, official rule text verbatim. The family is outside the CEN EN 16931 BR-* gap universes, so the fireable-missing == 0 claim for those universes is unaffected. test_coverage_gap.py recomputes this section live from the vendored .sch files and fails on any drift.

cvd_tmp_family.label: the Clean-Vehicle-Directive (BR-DE-CVD-*) and temporary (BR-TMP-*) rules of the official KoSIT XRechnung Schematron artifacts
cvd_tmp_family.description: Machine-checked enumeration of the Clean-Vehicle-Directive (BR-DE-CVD-*) and temporary (BR-TMP-*) rules of the official KoSIT XRechnung Schematron artifacts — the Clean-Vehicle-Directive profile asserts (gated in the artifacts behind the CVD CustomizationID …#compliant#urn:xeinkauf.de:kosit:xrechnung:cvd_0.9) plus the ungated temporary BR-TMP-* asserts — extracted by a real XML parse of sch:assert/@id from BOTH vendored KoSIT binding artifacts (id prefix BR-DE-CVD-/BR-TMP-; BR-DE-TMP-32 is NOT in this family — it belongs to the plain BR-DE CIUS layer and is implemented separately). The UBL artifact carries nine asserts; the CII artifact carries the same nine plus BR-TMP-3, which exists ONLY in the CII binding (the matrix therefore tags BR-TMP-3 syntax='cii', never 'both'). Implemented ids are read from the live einvoice.rules_xrechnung registries per binding and are differentially proven per binding (LEG 2 / LEG 4); each assert row carries the official flag so the severity-mirrors-the-artifact claim is machine-visible. test_coverage_gap.py recomputes this section live from the vendored .sch files and fails on any drift, so an artifact bump that adds or un-gates a CVD/TMP assert reopens the worklist automatically.
